carbon_policy_signal.item: EU ETS review scheduled July; Commission to revisit EUA floor price, free allocation rules, and carbon-leakage provisions amid Poland criticism.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: July policy review creates near-term regulatory uncertainty on fossil-fuel dispatch marginal cost and EUA curve shape; Q3 roll risk for desk positioning.

freshness_caveat: Coal and clean-dark spread are stale (161 and 156 days old respectively); merit-order ranking and dark-spread hedges unreliable pending data refresh.
risk_flags: Storage at 41.25% full, 14.2 pp below 5-yr seasonal — refill pace critical to H2 power risk and TTF upside.
Renewables at 80.2% (97th-percentile) are flattening peak spreads; demand-side response further compresses residual-load volatility.
ETS review scheduled July creates near-term regulatory uncertainty on EUA floor, free allocation, and carbon-leakage rules.
Coal data stale (161 days old, 7th-percentile); clean-dark spread stale (156 days); merit-order ranking unreliable pending refresh.

base.summary: Renewables suppress DA spreads; storage refill steady; TTF grinds near 50 EUR/MWh; DE power at median.

downside.summary: Mild weather, strong renewables ramp, and fiscal-driven capex acceleration compress power prices; storage fills ahead of schedule; TTF slides.

upside.summary: Geopolitical oil shock (Russian sanctions tightening) lifts Brent; crude-linked LNG contracts push TTF +5–8%; storage refill defers into H2.

themes: Renewable suppression of DA + peak-shaving demand response
Storage deficit and summer refill race
EU fiscal acceleration of capex + ETS review policy risk
Geopolitical oil sanctions and indirect LNG arb
top_drivers: Renewables at 97th-percentile (80.2% of load) crush spot merit order; AI-driven demand-side response compresses peak spreads and residual-load volatility.
EU storage 14.2 pp below seasonal (41.25% full, 17th-percentile) creates summer refill urgency; refill pace will set H2 power and TTF curve shape.
EU fiscal exemption of green capex accelerates wind/solar deployment and grid upgrades, pushing down power-generation marginal cost over weeks–months horizon.
ETS review in July introduces regulatory uncertainty on EUA floor and free allocation; indirect drag on fossil-fuel merit order and Q3 curve rolls.
top_takeaway: Renewables at 97th-percentile crush merit order; storage 14 pp below seasonal average sets H2 refill urgency amid demand surge from AI and peak-shaving compression.

watchlist.why: 41.25% full (17th-percentile) requires accelerated injection to avoid Q4 2026 tightness; TTF and DE power sensitivity to summer weather and LNG feed.

watchlist.metric: ETS floor price and free allocation policy
watchlist.why: July Commission review will shape EUA curve and industrial-dispatch marginal cost; desk positioning critical for Q3 expiry rolls.
US Gulf pipeline capacity +44.9 Bcf/d (2026–27) may compress Henry Hub–TTF, supporting EU storage refill and moderating TTF upside into winter.
AI demand-side response and household peak-shaving flatten residual load; reduced volatility pressures month-ahead and Cal curves.

watchlist_dated.name: EU trade deal final vote
watchlist_dated.why: Tariff outcome will signal transatlantic LNG flow risk and crude-linked gas arb sensitivity.

watchlist_dated.name: Commission ETS review
watchlist_dated.why: EUA floor, free allocation, and carbon-leakage rules revision; high desk relevance for Q3 curve positioning.
